steps.title: Identify the two segments of Bill's trip
steps.content: Bill's journey has two parts: (1) he initially drove 16.0 miles at an unspecified speed, and (2) then he drove at a constant speed of 60.0 miles per hour for 2 hours. We need the total distance from both segments.

steps.title: Calculate distance for the constant-speed segment
steps.content:
Use the formula distance = rate × time for the second segment of the trip.
$$\text{Distance} = 60.0 \text{ mph} \times 2 \text{ hours} = 120 \text{ miles}$$

steps.title: Sum both segments to find total distance
steps.content:
The total distance is the sum of the initial segment and the constant-speed segment:
$$\text{Total distance} = 16.0 + 120 = 136 \text{ miles}$$
